I've uploaded a python code repository in the directory . Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
Strings and Boxed types should be compared using "equals()"
These methods seemed to assume that in order to detect an unchanged argument (and to skip property change notification), identity comparison is appropriate. But this [would only work in rare circumstances](https://stackoverflow.com/a/32926523/7641). See also [SonarQube rule 4973](https://rules.sonarsource.com/java/type/Bug/RSPEC-4973).

Created from the PR https://github.com/javaparser/javaparser/pull/4790

</pr_description>

    35	/**
    36	 * The boolean literals.
    37	 * <br>{@code true}
    38	 * <br>{@code false}
    39	 *
    40	 * @author Julio Vilmar Gesser
    41	 */
    42	public class BooleanLiteralExpr extends LiteralExpr {
    43	
    44	    private boolean value;
    45	
    46	    public BooleanLiteralExpr() {
    47	        this(null, false);
    48	    }
    49	
    50	    @AllFieldsConstructor
    51	    public BooleanLiteralExpr(boolean value) {
    52	        this(null, value);
    53	    }
    54	
    55	    /**
    56	     * This constructor is used by the parser and is considered private.
    57	     */
    58	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.MainConstructorGenerator")
    59	    public BooleanLiteralExpr(TokenRange tokenRange, boolean value) {
    60	        super(tokenRange);
    61	        setValue(value);
    62	        customInitialization();
    63	    }
    64	
    65	    @Override
    66	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.AcceptGenerator")
    67	    public <R, A> R accept(final GenericVisitor<R, A> v, final A arg) {
    68	        return v.visit(this, arg);
    69	    }
    70	
    71	    @Override
    72	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.AcceptGenerator")
    73	    public <A> void accept(final VoidVisitor<A> v, final A arg) {
    74	        v.visit(this, arg);
    75	    }
    76	
    77	    /**
    78	     * The code generator likes to generate an "is" getter for boolean, so this here is the generated version,
    79	     * but "getValue" does the same and makes more sense.
    80	     */
    81	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.PropertyGenerator")
    82	    public boolean isValue() {
    83	        return value;
    84	    }
    85	
    86	    public boolean getValue() {
    87	        return isValue();
    88	    }
    89	
    90	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.PropertyGenerator")
    91	    public BooleanLiteralExpr setValue(final boolean value) {
    92	        if (value == this.value) {
    93	            return this;
    94	        }
    95	        notifyPropertyChange(ObservableProperty.VALUE, this.value, value);
    96	        this.value = value;
    97	        return this;
    98	    }
    99	
   100	    @Override
   101	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.CloneGenerator")
   102	    public BooleanLiteralExpr clone() {
   103	        return (BooleanLiteralExpr) accept(new CloneVisitor(), null);
   104	    }
   105	
   106	    @Override
   107	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.GetMetaModelGenerator")

   110	    }

   115	        return true;
   116	    }
   117

   122	    }

    31	import com.github.javaparser.metamodel.LiteralStringValueExprMetaModel;
    32	import java.util.Optional;
    33	import java.util.function.Consumer;
    34	
    35	/**
    36	 * Any literal value that is stored internally as a String.
    37	 */
    38	public abstract class LiteralStringValueExpr extends LiteralExpr {
    39	
    40	    protected String value;
    41	
    42	    @AllFieldsConstructor
    43	    public LiteralStringValueExpr(final String value) {
    44	        this(null, value);
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    /**
    48	     * This constructor is used by the parser and is considered private.
    49	     */
    50	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.MainConstructorGenerator")
    51	    public LiteralStringValueExpr(TokenRange tokenRange, String value) {
    52	        super(tokenRange);
    53	        setValue(value);
    54	        customInitialization();
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.PropertyGenerator")
    58	    public String getValue() {
    59	        return value;
    60	    }
    61	
    62	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.PropertyGenerator")
    63	    public LiteralStringValueExpr setValue(final String value) {
    64	        assertNotNull(value);
    65	        if (value == this.value) {
    66	            return this;
    67	        }
    68	        notifyPropertyChange(ObservableProperty.VALUE, this.value, value);
    69	        this.value = value;
    70	        return this;
    71	    }
    72	
    73	    @Override
    74	    @Generated("com.github.javaparser.generator.core.node.CloneGenerator")
    75	    public LiteralStringValueExpr clone() {
    76	        return (LiteralStringValueExpr) accept(new CloneVisitor(), null);
    77	    }

    65	
    66	    private void generateSetter(
    67	            BaseNodeMetaModel nodeMetaModel, ClassOrInterfaceDeclaration nodeCoid, PropertyMetaModel property) {
    68	        // Ensure the relevant imports have been added for the methods/annotations used
    69	        nodeCoid.findCompilationUnit().get().addImport(ObservableProperty.class);
    70	
    71	        final String name = property.getName();
    72	        // Fill body
    73	        final String observableName = camelCaseToScreaming(name.startsWith("is") ? name.substring(2) : name);
    74	        declaredProperties.put(observableName, property);
    75	
    76	        if (property == JavaParserMetaModel.nodeMetaModel.commentPropertyMetaModel) {
    77	            // Node.comment has a very specific setter that we shouldn't overwrite.
    78	            return;
    79	        }
    80	
    81	        final MethodDeclaration setter = new MethodDeclaration(
    82	                createModifierList(PUBLIC),
    83	                parseType(property.getContainingNodeMetaModel().getTypeNameGenerified()),
    84	                property.getSetterMethodName());
    85	        annotateWhenOverridden(nodeMetaModel, setter);
    86	        if (property.getContainingNodeMetaModel().hasWildcard()) {
    87	            setter.setType(parseType("T"));
    88	        }
    89	        setter.addAndGetParameter(property.getTypeNameForSetter(), property.getName())
    90	                .addModifier(FINAL);
    91	
    92	        final BlockStmt body = setter.getBody().get();
    93	        body.getStatements().clear();

package com.github.javaparser.utils;

import java.util.HashMap;
import java.util.Map;

public class ClassUtils {

    /**
     * Maps primitive {@code Class}es to their corresponding wrapper {@code Class}.
     */
    private static final Map<Class<?>, Class<?>> primitiveWrapperMap = new HashMap<>();

    static {
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Boolean.TYPE, Boolean.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Byte.TYPE, Byte.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Character.TYPE, Character.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Short.TYPE, Short.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Integer.TYPE, Integer.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Long.TYPE, Long.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Double.TYPE, Double.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Float.TYPE, Float.class);
        primitiveWrapperMap.put(Void.TYPE, Void.TYPE);
    }

    /**
     * Maps wrapper {@code Class}es to their corresponding primitive types.
     */
    private static final Map<Class<?>, Class<?>> wrapperPrimitiveMap = new HashMap<>();

    static {
        for (final Class<?> primitiveClass : primitiveWrapperMap.keySet()) {
            final Class<?> wrapperClass = primitiveWrapperMap.get(primitiveClass);
            if (!primitiveClass.equals(wrapperClass)) {
                wrapperPrimitiveMap.put(wrapperClass, primitiveClass);
            }
        }
    }

    /**
     * Returns whether the given {@code type} is a primitive or primitive wrapper ({@link Boolean}, {@link Byte},
     * {@link Character},
     * {@link Short}, {@link Integer}, {@link Long}, {@link Double}, {@link Float}).
     *
     * @param type The class to query or null.
     * @return true if the given {@code type} is a primitive or primitive wrapper ({@link Boolean}, {@link Byte}, {@link
     * Character}, {@link Short}, {@link Integer}, {@link Long}, {@link Double}, {@link Float}).
     */
    public static boolean isPrimitiveOrWrapper(final Class<?> type) {
        if (type == null) {
            return false;
        }
        return type.isPrimitive() || isPrimitiveWrapper(type);
    }

    /**
     * Returns whether the given {@code type} is a primitive wrapper ({@link Boolean}, {@link Byte}, {@link Character},
     * {@link Short},
     * {@link Integer}, {@link Long}, {@link Double}, {@link Float}).
     *
     * @param type The class to query or null.
     * @return true if the given {@code type} is a primitive wrapper ({@link Boolean}, {@link Byte}, {@link Character},
     * {@link Short}, {@link Integer}, {@link Long}, {@link Double}, {@link Float}).
     * @since 3.1
     */
    public static boolean isPrimitiveWrapper(final Class<?> type) {
        return wrapperPrimitiveMap.containsKey(type);
    }
}

2025-12-26 04:22:11,590 - INFO - swea-agent - 💭 THOUGHT
Empty text blockI've updated the codebase to ensure that Strings and boxed types are compared using `equals()` (or more safely, `Objects.equals()`) instead of identity comparison (`==`). This follows the recommendations in the PR description and prevents issues where identity comparison might fail for these types.

The following changes were made:
1.  **Modified `javaparser-core/src/main/java/com/github/javaparser/ast/expr/LiteralStringValueExpr.java`**: Changed the `setValue` method to use `Objects.equals()` for comparing the new and old String values. This ensures that change notifications are only skipped when the Strings are actually equal in content.
2.  **Modified `javaparser-core-generators/src/main/java/com/github/javaparser/generator/core/node/PropertyGenerator.java`**: Updated the generator responsible for producing setter methods across the AST. It now generates code using `Objects.equals()` for all attribute types that are not primitives (which includes `String` and boxed types like `Integer`). Node types and primitives continue to use `==` for performance and correctness.
3.  **Modified `javaparser-core/src/main/java/com/github/javaparser/printer/lexicalpreservation/LexicalPreservingPrinter.java`**: Updated the internal `Observer` within the lexical preservation logic to use `Objects.equals()` when comparing property values. This ensures that the printer correctly identifies whether a property has truly changed, avoiding skipped updates when String or Boxed values are different instances but have the same value.

These changes ensure the repository correctly handles value equality for critical comparisons in setters and observers, satisfying the requirements of the PR.



🎬 ACTION
submit
